title: Pre-playthrough priority pass
summary: Thirty-ninth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This packet narrows the remaining pre-playthrough token budget to four jobs: lane 1 should make the first session feel self-contained and standalone, including a late tiny menu-focus follow-up after critique, lane 4 should deepen Root Hollow with one evidence-backed middle leg instead of applying an older field-case patch literally, lane 3 should polish canopy and cave readability instead of widening geometry again, and lane 2 should stay in review-first freeze mode unless it finds a real blocker in active route content.

execution_order.goal: Close or reopen lane 2 with the smallest possible pre-playthrough budget.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 2 should only keep moving before the playthrough if there is a real blocker in active route content.

execution_order.goal: Make the first session understandable without external docs.
execution_order.why_now: A self-contained opening will make the playthrough feedback far more reliable.

execution_order.goal: Review the first-session polish from a fresh-player perspective.
execution_order.why_now: This is the fastest way to catch confusing onboarding before the human run.

execution_order.goal: Default menu focus to the first travel or support action so the taught keyboard path is truthful.
execution_order.why_now: The standalone copy is better, but the live keyboard default still lands on helper toggles instead of the route-support actions.

execution_order.goal: Review the menu default-focus follow-up for fresh-playthrough clarity.
execution_order.why_now: The menu should now reward the exact `M` then `Enter` path the player has just been taught.

execution_order.goal: Polish the current canopy and cave family for readability and recovery.
execution_order.why_now: Those spaces are already a strength, but they still have the biggest disorientation risk in a full playthrough.

execution_order.goal: Review the vertical polish pass for playthrough readiness.
execution_order.why_now: The lane should prove the spaces are readable before widening them any further.

execution_order.goal: Add one evidence-backed middle leg to Root Hollow's chapter loop.
execution_order.why_now: This is the most valuable remaining gameplay push from the external handoff that still fits the current live Route v2 architecture.

execution_order.goal: Review the Root Hollow follow-up for calm chapter feel and handheld readability.
execution_order.why_now: The expedition should feel deeper, but not noisier or more system-heavy, before the playthrough.

execution_order.goal: Normalize legacy Root Hollow progress to the new four-leg chapter shape.
execution_order.why_now: The pre-playthrough chapter follow-up is clean for fresh saves, but older in-progress expedition states now need a truthful recovery path.

execution_order.goal: Review the Root Hollow legacy-progress normalization.
execution_order.why_now: Save recoverability is a hard gate, so the cleanup pass needs a final check before lane 4 stands down.

execution_order.goal: Trim the Root Hollow request summary back under the shared notebook budget.
execution_order.why_now: The legacy-save normalization is clean, but lane 4 still leaves the shared content-quality suite red on a 101-character field-request summary.

execution_order.goal: Review the Root Hollow notebook-budget follow-up.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 4 should stand down only once the four-leg chapter copy is truthful and the shared compact-copy guardrail is green again.

guardrails: Do not spend pre-playthrough budget on a fourth route, a second expedition card, a bigger archive shell, or a larger station dashboard.
Do not apply the external field-case patch literally where current Route v2 code already supersedes it.
Prefer contained file-level polish and truthful copy over new surface area.
Keep the game cozy, science-first, and readable at 256x160.
acceptance_focus: A fresh player can find the journal, world map, field station, and active route without outside explanation.
The default menu focus supports the taught keyboard path instead of dropping onto helper toggles first.
Root Hollow feels more like an authored chapter without opening a new UI or system family.
Legacy in-progress Root Hollow saves recover truthfully into the new chapter shape.
The current canopy and cave family feels readable enough to judge fairly in a full playthrough.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, your lane brief, docs/reports/2026-03-31-pre-playthrough-priority-pass.md, and this packet. Treat this as a pre-playthrough polish pass: improve standalone clarity, expedition truth and depth, and vertical readability while explicitly avoiding broader expansion that can wait until after the human playthrough.
